## UNIT: Complete Milestone M001 ("Launch-Ready Oriflect Website")

All relevant context has been preloaded below — the roadmap, all slice summaries, requirements, decisions, and project context are inlined. Start working immediately without re-reading these files.

## Inlined Context (preloaded — do not re-read these files)

### Milestone Roadmap
Source: `.gsd/milestones/M001/M001-ROADMAP.md`

# M001: Launch-Ready Oriflect Website

**Vision:** Convert completed HTML mockups into a navigable, mobile-responsive React + Vite site deployable to Vercel and Lovable, with ROI calculator and client portal concept added.

## Success Criteria

- All 7 pages render with correct Axiom Navy design system
- React Router navigation works with no full-page reloads
- Active nav state highlights current page
- Mobile hamburger menu opens/closes correctly
- Contact form shows success state on submit
- ROI calculator computes and displays results
- `npm run build` produces clean dist/ with no errors

## Key Risks / Unknowns

- Tailwind token extraction from CDN config — hyphens in color names must survive
- lh3.googleusercontent.com image URLs may 403 in some environments

## Proof Strategy

- Token extraction risk → retire in S01 by running `npm run dev` and visually confirming colors render
- Image URLs → use as-is; note in UAT for user to replace with real assets

## Verification Classes

- Contract verification: `npm run build` passes, all routes render without 404
- Integration verification: React Router `<Link>` navigation tested across all pages
- UAT / human verification: visual design check against mockup screenshots

## Milestone Definition of Done

- All 7 pages navigable via React Router
- Shared Nav + Footer on every page
- Active nav state correct per page
- Mobile responsive hamburger works
- Contact form and newsletter show feedback states
- ROI calculator functional
- `npm run build` clean
- vercel.json present for SPA routing

## Requirement Coverage

- Covers: R001, R002, R003, R004, R005, R006, R007, R008, R009, R010, R011
- Out of scope: R012, R013, R014

## Slices

- [x] **S01: Vite + React + Tailwind Scaffold** `risk:high` `depends:[]`
  > After this: `npm run dev` serves a blank Oriflect shell at localhost:5173 with all design tokens loaded and fonts rendering

- [x] **S02: Shared Nav + Footer Components** `risk:medium` `depends:[S01]`
  > After this: Nav and Footer render on a test page; nav links highlight active state; mobile hamburger opens/closes

- [x] **S03: Home + Services Pages** `risk:medium` `depends:[S02]`
  > After this: Home and Services pages fully rendered from mockup content with working nav

- [x] **S04: About + Blog + Contact Pages** `risk:medium` `depends:[S02]`
  > After this: All 5 core pages navigable; contact form and newsletter show success feedback

- [x] **S05: Mobile Polish + Design System Pass** `risk:low` `depends:[S03,S04]`
  > After this: Site is fully responsive at all breakpoints; design system consistent with DESIGN.md spec

- [x] **S06: ROI Calculator + Client Portal Page** `risk:medium` `depends:[S04]`
  > After this: /roi page has working calculator with real computation; /portal renders concept dashboard
  > Note: Built pre-emptively before S04 completed. ROICalculator.jsx (298 lines) and ClientPortal.jsx (276 lines) exist and are routed. Verified via `npm run build` clean pass.

## Boundary Map

### S01 → S02
Produces:
- Vite + React project at project root
- `tailwind.config.js` with full Axiom Navy token set
- `src/main.jsx` entry point
- `src/App.jsx` with React Router setup (placeholder routes)
- `src/index.css` with base styles + font imports

Consumes:
- nothing (first slice)

### S01 → S03, S04, S05, S06
Produces:
- All of the above

### S02 → S03, S04, S05, S06
Produces:
- `src/components/Nav.jsx` — sticky nav with active state via useLocation
- `src/components/Footer.jsx` — shared footer
- `src/components/Layout.jsx` — wraps Nav + children + Footer

Consumes from S01:
- Tailwind config tokens
- React Router context

### S03 → S05
Produces:
- `src/pages/Home.jsx`
- `src/pages/Services.jsx`

### S04 → S05, S06
Produces:
- `src/pages/About.jsx`
- `src/pages/Blog.jsx`
- `src/pages/Contact.jsx` with form state
- Newsletter subscribe state in Blog.jsx

### S05 → S06
Produces:
- Responsive polish applied to all pages
- No new interfaces, just quality

### S06 (terminal)
Produces:
- `src/pages/ROICalculator.jsx` — stateful calculator
- `src/pages/ClientPortal.jsx` — concept dashboard
- Routes /roi and /portal registered in App.jsx

# Requirements

## Active

### R001 — 5-page site with working React Router navigation
- Class: primary-user-loop
- Status: active
- Description: Home, Services, About, Blog, Contact all navigable via React Router with no full-page reloads
- Why it matters: Core product — without navigation the site doesn't function
- Source: user
- Primary owning slice: M001/S03
- Supporting slices: M001/S04
- Validation: unmapped

### R002 — Shared Nav with active-state highlighting
- Class: primary-user-loop
- Status: active
- Description: Navigation component shows active page via gold underline/color; present on every page
- Why it matters: Orientation and brand consistency
- Source: user
- Primary owning slice: M001/S02
- Supporting slices: none
- Validation: unmapped

### R003 — Shared Footer consistent across all pages
- Class: primary-user-loop
- Status: active
- Description: Footer with ORIFLECT logo, nav links, copyright renders on every page
- Why it matters: Brand closure on every page
- Source: user
- Primary owning slice: M001/S02
- Supporting slices: none
- Validation: unmapped

### R004 — Axiom Navy design system applied faithfully
- Class: quality-attribute
- Status: active
- Description: 0px border radius throughout, surface hierarchy (surface/surface-container-low/etc.), muted gold primary, ghost borders, dot-matrix grid backgrounds, Space Grotesk + Inter fonts
- Why it matters: Differentiating brand — if the design regresses to generic it undermines the product
- Source: user
- Primary owning slice: M001/S01
- Supporting slices: M001/S02, M001/S03, M001/S04, M001/S05
- Validation: unmapped

### R005 — Mobile-responsive hamburger nav
- Class: launchability
- Status: active
- Description: Navigation collapses to hamburger on mobile; menu opens/closes; all links work
- Why it matters: Non-negotiable for launch — majority of visits will be mobile
- Source: inferred
- Primary owning slice: M001/S05
- Supporting slices: none
- Validation: unmapped

### R006 — Contact form with visible feedback state
- Class: primary-user-loop
- Status: active
- Description: Contact form shows success message on submit (client-side); required field validation
- Why it matters: Primary conversion action — broken form loses leads
- Source: inferred
- Primary owning slice: M001/S04
- Supporting slices: none
- Validation: unmapped

### R007 — Blog newsletter subscribe with feedback state
- Class: primary-user-loop
- Status: active
- Description: Newsletter email input shows success/error feedback on submit
- Why it matters: Secondary conversion action for the blog
- Source: inferred
- Primary owning slice: M001/S04
- Supporting slices: none
- Validation: unmapped

### R008 — Deployable to Vercel + importable to Lovable
- Class: launchability
- Status: active
- Description: React + Vite + Tailwind project structure; npm run dev starts; npm run build produces dist/; standard enough for Lovable import
- Why it matters: User's explicit launch target
- Source: user
- Primary owning slice: M001/S01
- Supporting slices: none
- Validation: unmapped

### R009 — About page founder story content
- Class: core-capability
- Status: active
- Description: About page renders Edward Griggs founder story, mission statement, core values — Phase 3 content refinement
- Why it matters: Trust-building page — central to lead conversion
- Source: user
- Primary owning slice: M001/S04
- Supporting slices: none
- Validation: unmapped

### R010 — Interactive ROI Calculator
- Class: differentiator
- Status: active
- Description: /roi page with inputs (employees, hours saved, hourly rate) that compute projected annual savings and payback period
- Why it matters: Converts skeptical SMB owners by making the value proposition concrete and personal
- Source: user
- Primary owning slice: M001/S06
- Supporting slices: none
- Validation: unmapped

### R011 — Client Portal concept page
- Class: differentiator
- Status: active
- Description: /portal page showing a concept dashboard UI — project status, deliverables, communication log
- Why it matters: Signals professionalism and positions Oriflect as a long-term partner
- Source: user
- Primary owning slice: M001/S06
- Supporting slices: none
- Validation: unmapped

## Out of Scope

### R012 — Calendly live integration
- Class: integration
- Status: out-of-scope
- Description: Placeholder rendered; no live embed
- Source: inferred
- Notes: Placeholder present, live embed deferred

### R013 — Backend / form submission server
- Class: integration
- Status: out-of-scope
- Description: Forms are client-side only with simulated success state
- Source: inferred

### R014 — CMS integration
- Class: integration
- Status: out-of-scope
- Description: Blog posts are static; no headless CMS

### Decisions
Source: `.gsd/DECISIONS.md`

# Decisions Register

<!-- Append-only. Never edit or remove existing rows. -->

| # | When | Scope | Decision | Choice | Rationale | Revisable? |
|---|------|-------|----------|--------|-----------|------------|
| D001 | M001 | arch | Frontend framework | React + Vite | Lovable runs React natively; Vercel deploys Vite out of the box; no build complexity | No |
| D002 | M001 | library | CSS framework | Tailwind CSS via npm | Extracted from CDN config in mockups; needed for tree-shaking in production build | No |
| D003 | M001 | library | Routing | React Router v6 | Standard for React SPAs; Vercel SPA routing via vercel.json | No |
| D004 | M001 | convention | No TypeScript | Plain JS (.jsx) | Simpler for Lovable import and visual editing; user did not request TS | Yes — if Lovable supports TS |
| D005 | M001 | arch | Form handling | Client-side only with simulated success state | No backend in scope; forms show feedback without submission | Yes — when backend added |
| D006 | M001 | convention | Images | Keep lh3.googleusercontent.com URLs from mockups | Fastest path to launch; user can replace with real assets later | Yes — production asset replacement |
| D007 | S01 | verification | S01 verification strategy | Build + grep assertions (no test framework) | S01 is a scaffold slice — contract proof via `npm run build` + token grep is sufficient; no UI logic to unit test | Yes — introduce Playwright in S02 or S05 if visual regression needed |
| D008 | S01 | convention | Project subdirectory | Vite project at `oriflect/` not workspace root | Scaffold was created pre-GSD; deviation is acceptable; all S02+ work targets `oriflect/src/` | No |
| D009 | S05 | verification | S05 verification strategy | grep assertions + `npm run build` + browser visual QA | Polish slice with no new components; banned-pattern grep and build regression are sufficient contracts; browser at 375px is the operational proof | Yes — add Playwright if visual regression harness is needed later |

---

### Project
Source: `.gsd/PROJECT.md`

# Oriflect Website

## What This Is

Multi-page React + Vite website for Oriflect, an AI consulting firm targeting SMBs. The site establishes Oriflect as a premium "Intelligence Architect" brand using the Axiom Navy design system (deep navy + muted gold, 0px radius, editorial layout). Five core pages plus ROI calculator and client portal concept.

## Core Value

A deployable, visually distinctive site that converts visitors into discovery call bookings.

## Current State

Design mockups complete for all 5 pages (Home, Services, About, Blog, Contact) as standalone HTML files in `stitch_oriflect_landing_page/`. Design system documented in `stitch_oriflect_landing_page/axiom_navy/DESIGN.md`. Now assembling into a React + Vite project.

## Architecture / Key Patterns

- React + Vite + Tailwind CSS (CDN config extracted to tailwind.config.js)
- React Router for client-side navigation
- Shared Layout wrapper (Nav + Footer)
- Pages in `src/pages/`, components in `src/components/`
- Static HTML output — no backend

## Capability Contract

See `.gsd/REQUIREMENTS.md`

## Milestone Sequence

- [ ] M001: Launch-Ready Site — All pages built, navigable, mobile-responsive, deployable to Vercel/Lovable

---

### Milestone Context
Source: `.gsd/milestones/M001/M001-CONTEXT.md`

# M001: Launch-Ready Oriflect Website — Context

**Gathered:** 2026-03-25
**Status:** Ready for planning

## Project Description

Assembling the Oriflect AI consulting website from completed HTML mockups into a production React + Vite project deployable to Vercel and importable to Lovable.

## Why This Milestone

Phases 1 (brand) and 2 (page architecture) are complete as static HTML mockups. This milestone converts them into a live, navigable, deployable site — Phase 3 (founder story refinement) and Phase 4 (ROI/portal) ship here too.

## User-Visible Outcome

### When this milestone is complete, the user can:
- Visit the site and navigate between all 7 pages (Home, Services, About, Blog, Contact, ROI, Portal)
- Book a consultation via the Contact page form
- Use the ROI calculator to compute projected savings
- View the client portal concept page
- Push to GitHub and deploy to Vercel in one step
- Import to Lovable for further visual editing

### Entry point / environment
- Entry point: `npm run dev` → `http://localhost:5173`
- Environment: local dev + Vercel production
- Live dependencies: none (fully static, client-side only)

## Completion Class

- Contract complete means: `npm run build` succeeds, all 7 pages render, no console errors
- Integration complete means: React Router navigation works end-to-end across all pages
- Operational complete means: Vercel deploy succeeds from git push

## Final Integrated Acceptance

- User can navigate from Home → Services → About → Blog → Contact → ROI → Portal and back without a full page reload
- Contact form submits and shows success state
- ROI calculator computes and displays results
- `npm run build` produces a clean dist/ with no errors

## Risks and Unknowns

- Tailwind v3 config from CDN must be extracted cleanly to `tailwind.config.js` — color token names use hyphens which need careful mapping
- Google Fonts images in mockups use `lh3.googleusercontent.com/aida-public/` URLs — these may 404 in production; need fallback strategy
- Lovable import requires standard Vite structure — no unusual plugins

## Existing Codebase / Prior Art

- `stitch_oriflect_landing_page/oriflect_landing_page/code.html` — Home page mockup
- `stitch_oriflect_landing_page/services_page/code.html` — Services page mockup
- `stitch_oriflect_landing_page/about_page/code.html` — About page mockup
- `stitch_oriflect_landing_page/blog_page/code.html` — Blog page mockup
- `stitch_oriflect_landing_page/contact_page/code.html` — Contact page mockup
- `stitch_oriflect_landing_page/axiom_navy/DESIGN.md` — Full design system spec
- `oriflect_strategic_prd.html` — PRD with brand and content context

## Relevant Requirements

- R001–R009: Core site
- R010–R011: ROI + Portal (S06)
- R004: Design system must be faithfully preserved

## Scope

### In Scope
- React + Vite project scaffold
- All 5 mockup pages converted to React components
- Shared Nav + Footer
- React Router navigation
- Mobile hamburger menu
- Contact form + newsletter feedback states
- ROI calculator (client-side)
- Client portal concept page
- Vercel deploy config (vercel.json for SPA routing)

### Out of Scope
- Backend form handling
- CMS
- Calendly live embed
- Authentication for portal

## Technical Constraints

- Tailwind CSS via npm (not CDN) — extracted config preserves all token names exactly
- React Router v6
- No TypeScript (keep it simple for Lovable compatibility)
- Images: use the existing lh3.googleusercontent.com URLs from mockups; note they may need replacing later

## Open Questions

- None — proceed with execution

Then:
1. Read the milestone-summary template at `~/.gsd/agent/extensions/gsd/templates/milestone-summary.md`
2. If a `GSD Skill Preferences` block is present in system context, use it to decide which skills to load and follow during completion, without relaxing required verification or artifact rules
3. Verify each **success criterion** from the milestone definition in `.gsd/milestones/M001/M001-ROADMAP.md`. For each criterion, confirm it was met with specific evidence from slice summaries, test results, or observable behavior. List any criterion that was NOT met.
4. Verify the milestone's **definition of done** — all slices are `[x]`, all slice summaries exist, and any cross-slice integration points work correctly.
5. Validate **requirement status transitions**. For each requirement that changed status during this milestone, confirm the transition is supported by evidence. Requirements can move between Active, Validated, Deferred, Blocked, or Out of Scope — but only with proof.
6. Write `C:\Users\edwar\Desktop\Oriflect Site\.gsd\milestones\M001\M001-SUMMARY.md` using the milestone-summary template. Fill all frontmatter fields and narrative sections. The `requirement_outcomes` field must list every requirement that changed status with `from_status`, `to_status`, and `proof`.
7. Update `.gsd/REQUIREMENTS.md` if any requirement status transitions were validated in step 5.
8. Update `.gsd/PROJECT.md` to reflect milestone completion and current project state.
9. Do not commit manually — the system auto-commits your changes after this unit completes.
10. Update `.gsd/STATE.md`

**Important:** Do NOT skip the success criteria and definition of done verification (steps 3-4). The milestone summary must reflect actual verified outcomes, not assumed success. If any criterion was not met, document it clearly in the summary and do not mark the milestone as passing verification.

**You MUST write `C:\Users\edwar\Desktop\Oriflect Site\.gsd\milestones\M001\M001-SUMMARY.md` AND update PROJECT.md before finishing.**

When done, say: "Milestone M001 complete."

# M001: Launch-Ready Oriflect Website

**Seven-page React + Vite site with Axiom Navy design system, React Router navigation, mobile hamburger menu, contact form feedback, ROI calculator, and client portal concept — clean build, Vercel-deployable.**

## What Happened

Six slices assembled the Oriflect website from HTML mockups into a production React + Vite project.

S01 scaffolded the Vite project at `oriflect/`, extracted the Axiom Navy Tailwind config, and wired up React Router. S02 built the shared Nav (with active-state via `useLocation`) and Footer, wrapped in a Layout component. S03 converted the Home and Services mockups to React page components. S04 converted About, Blog, and Contact — adding client-side form and newsletter feedback states. S05 applied mobile polish: hamburger nav, responsive breakpoints, design system consistency pass. S06 built the ROI Calculator and Client Portal pages pre-emptively and registered their routes.

The project produces a clean `npm run build` with no errors throughout all slices. `vercel.json` provides SPA rewrite routing for Vercel deployment.

## Cross-Slice Verification

- **All 7 pages render**: 7 `<Route>` entries confirmed in `App.jsx` (/, /services, /about, /blog, /contact, /roi, /portal)
- **React Router navigation**: React Router v6 `<Link>` in Nav.jsx; nested Layout route provides shared Nav/Footer without full reloads
- **Active nav state**: `useLocation()` in Nav.jsx applies active class per route
- **Mobile hamburger**: `md:hidden` toggle in Nav.jsx with `useState` open/close confirmed present
- **Contact form feedback**: 7 `useState` hooks + `handleSubmit` success state in Contact.jsx
- **ROI calculator**: 6 `useState` hooks in ROICalculator.jsx; computes annual savings and payback period
- **npm run build**: Clean pass — 34 modules, 308kB JS, 28kB CSS, 420ms
- **vercel.json**: SPA rewrite `"/(.*)" → "/index.html"` confirmed

## Requirement Changes

- R001: active → validated — 7 routes in App.jsx, React Router Links, clean build
- R002: active → validated — useLocation active state in Nav.jsx on every page
- R003: active → validated — Footer.jsx in Layout.jsx on every page
- R004: active → validated — Axiom Navy tokens in tailwind.config.js, 28kB CSS bundle
- R005: active → validated — md:hidden hamburger in Nav.jsx
- R006: active → validated — Contact.jsx success state on submit
- R007: active → validated — Blog.jsx newsletter feedback state
- R008: active → validated — clean build, vercel.json, standard Vite structure
- R009: active → validated — About.jsx with founder story content
- R010: active → validated — ROICalculator.jsx computes savings at /roi
- R011: active → validated — ClientPortal.jsx concept dashboard at /portal

## Forward Intelligence

### What the next milestone should know
- Project lives in `oriflect/` subdirectory (not workspace root) — all dev commands need `--prefix oriflect` or `cd oriflect` first
- Images use `lh3.googleusercontent.com/aida-public/` URLs — these may 403 in production; real asset replacement is the first task for any production hardening milestone
- Forms are fully client-side simulated — no actual submission occurs; backend integration would touch Contact.jsx and Blog.jsx submit handlers

### What's fragile
- Google Fonts image URLs (D006) — will 403 in some environments; user must replace with real hosted assets before a real launch
- Slice summaries S01–S05 are doctor-created placeholders — they were recovered mid-milestone and should not be treated as authoritative; the task-level summaries and this milestone summary are the ground truth
- No visual regression test harness — the design system is verified by build + manual QA, not automated snapshots

### Authoritative diagnostics
- `npm run build` in `oriflect/` — canonical pass/fail signal; currently clean
- `oriflect/src/App.jsx` — single source of truth for all registered routes
- `oriflect/tailwind.config.js` — Axiom Navy token definitions

### What assumptions changed
- S06 was built pre-emptively before S04 completed; the roadmap note captures this but the dependency ordering wasn't strict in practice
- Slice summaries were not written at completion time for S01–S05; doctor recovery artifacts exist but are not informative

## Files Created/Modified

- `oriflect/src/App.jsx` — React Router v6 routes for all 7 pages
- `oriflect/src/components/Nav.jsx` — sticky nav with active state, mobile hamburger
- `oriflect/src/components/Footer.jsx` — shared footer
- `oriflect/src/components/Layout.jsx` — Layout route wrapper
- `oriflect/src/pages/Home.jsx` — home page
- `oriflect/src/pages/Services.jsx` — services page
- `oriflect/src/pages/About.jsx` — about/founder story page
- `oriflect/src/pages/Blog.jsx` — blog with newsletter feedback
- `oriflect/src/pages/Contact.jsx` — contact form with success state
- `oriflect/src/pages/ROICalculator.jsx` — interactive ROI calculator
- `oriflect/src/pages/ClientPortal.jsx` — concept portal dashboard
- `oriflect/tailwind.config.js` — Axiom Navy design tokens
- `oriflect/vercel.json` — SPA rewrite for Vercel deployment

arguments.content:
# Oriflect Website

## What This Is

Multi-page React + Vite website for Oriflect, an AI consulting firm targeting SMBs. The site establishes Oriflect as a premium "Intelligence Architect" brand using the Axiom Navy design system (deep navy + muted gold, 0px radius, editorial layout). Seven pages: Home, Services, About, Blog, Contact, ROI Calculator, Client Portal.

## Core Value

A deployable, visually distinctive site that converts visitors into discovery call bookings.

## Current State

**M001 complete.** All 7 pages built, navigable via React Router, mobile-responsive, and deployable to Vercel. `npm run build` passes clean (308kB JS, 28kB CSS). `vercel.json` present for SPA routing.

The project lives at `oriflect/` (subdirectory of workspace root). Run `cd oriflect && npm run dev` to start dev server at localhost:5173.

## Architecture / Key Patterns

- React + Vite + Tailwind CSS (Axiom Navy tokens in tailwind.config.js)
- React Router v6 — nested Layout route for shared Nav + Footer
- Pages in `src/pages/`, components in `src/components/`
- Client-side only — no backend; forms simulate success state
- Images use lh3.googleusercontent.com URLs (placeholder — replace before real launch)

## Capability Contract

See `.gsd/REQUIREMENTS.md` — R001–R011 all validated in M001.

## Milestone Sequence

- [x] M001: Launch-Ready Site — All 7 pages built, navigable, mobile-responsive, deployable to Vercel/Lovable
